for_c_level: Risk posture is low with full pass confirming production readiness for secure federated deployments; customer claims of reliable mTLS memory sharing are viable. No changes or regressions from prior runs, affirming stability post-patch2. Proceed with confidence in scaling to enterprise use.
for_non_technical: In this test, AI agents reliably shared and accessed memories with each other without any problems. The security setup ensured only authorized agents could interact, and all data remained consistent across the system. Overall, agents can depend on this setup to remember and retrieve shared information effectively.
for_sme: All tested scenarios (S1, S1b, S2, S4-S6, S9-S18, S20-S25, S28-S42) passed without failure modes, impacting primitives like write/recall, link/delete, bulk import/export, and hybrid search under mTLS. No primitives affected negatively; probable root cause analysis unnecessary due to clean execution. Testbook probes F# all green with no skips.
headline: Hermes v0.6.2 passes all mTLS federation tests cleanly.
what_it_proved: Demonstrated flawless replication, consistency, and security of agent-shared memories in a federated mTLS environment with no skips or failures.
what_it_tested: Exercised 35 scenarios testing memory writes, recalls, links, deletions, bulk operations, and security features across HTTP transport in a 4-node mTLS federation, covering core primitives like consolidation, versioning, and pub/sub.
